item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: As the image is not CC compliant, it has since been removed.

item.title: Image provenance
item.content:encoded:
This is a photo of Mabios De M'Paka, a member of the Société des Ambianceurs et des Personnes Élégantes (a fashion club in Brazzaville, Congo), taken in 2009 by Francesco Giusti.

Mr. Giusti's site says that all rights are reserved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this quite a bit, but there are some tone errors that I'm not a fan of: "from where the extra mass comes is unknown" in particular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. The man is clearly aware that his eyes are abnormal. He looks inside his own mouth for god's sake and worked in a circus. They have makeup artists, mirrors…the guy has had ample opportunity to see himself in the mirror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Berggren gave me permission on chat to replace the image with a photoshopped version. His eyelids are pretty much completely gone and the sewn Xs are faintly visible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not see the reason to deny returning the SCP its hat, but seeing that Mr. Fishbones is not aware of how his eyes look like, letting him watch himself would not be a greatest move from the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the first Herman-Fuller skip I've read, I found this well-written and oddly sad. I find the requests conducive to the overall effect and would encourage the author to keep them in. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
^That. It poses no risk and his comments reviewing his own work might lend an insight.

For that matter, why are the requests listed at all? That seems unnecesary and a bit retro, not really a bad thing, just doesn't add anything either.

I read once upon a time how circus elephants are trained to not run away. A baby elephant is tied by a heavy chain to a massive beam. It tries and tries to get away, break the chain, slip the cuff, uproot the beam, anythign and everything possible. Then it learns it can't and never tries again. From then on, a man lightly holding a bit of string can lead that elephant, because it -knows- it can't get away when there is something around it's ankle.

Fishbones made me feel like someone used the same sort of conditioning on him. Now he has Universal Stockholm Syndrome, not just resigned to captivity, but happy to perform for anyone who will "take him in".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• To watch the recordings of its performances (Denied)
  • To have its hat back (Denied)

Why were those denied?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ew. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, it's the herman-fuller tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a tag for this circus? This is the first skip I've seen from it and would love to read more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No offense to this article in particular, but as someone who wasn't much enthused with Hellerman's to begin with, the stream of circus skips is really starting to wear thin…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The time isn't exact - it's rough and he doesn't know exactly when it's coming, but it's around 7:30, so that's why he asked for the watch.

E: rephrase made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree; "causes no display of pain" implies that it does hurt and he's just holding it back, to me. It's easy enough to ask, "Hey, does it hurt when you do that?" and get a "nope" in return.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Approximately every 24 hours at 7:30 PM

A daily exact time isn't approximate.

The use of its ability is noiseless and painless;

I don't like the wording, maybe "and causes no display of pain"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changing the instances of he/him/his to it/its makes this sit much better with me. As far as I've always understood, SCPs are to be regarded by those working with them as objects and things, not people.

It's still kind of jarring to me, though, to go from reading:
"Where its eyes should be"
"it does not have eyeballs or eye sockets"
"lack of features in the ocular region"
to:
"SCP-1834 is able to elongate its eyes"

Buuut…I guess I know what you meant. Xstalks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect blend of disturbing, charming and off-kilter. Nice to see others working on the Circus now too. I too got a Little Mister vibe off of him, so that was a nice surprise in the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wholeheartedly agree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's less the clothes, more his pose. That's the pose of a lifetime performer at his peak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps I'll take a crack at it in the in the old 'toshop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! And no, his clothes don't change when at showtime — he requested that the recovery team take that picture before he was remanded into containment. Just the kind of guy he is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume that picture was taken at performance time, because that dude is suave-looking. To the point, I enjoyed this despite not having read any of the other Circus articles, which is how to do a theme series right in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure, sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small edit. You don't need to specify "three-dimensional polyhedra", as polyhedra are three-dimensional by nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author could stand to change most (if not all) of the "he"s to "it"s, and fix that line too. But otherwise this is solid, and I upvoted for the last line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a feeling I would like this as soon as I saw the picture, and the rest just proved that. Take my upvote, and my firstborn child.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. This is wonderful. You totally nailed the Circus' M.O. and particular flavor of weirdness. The effect is really fun and unique, and Mr. Fishbones himself is well-characterized. I especially like how he ended up with a very positive view of the circus and waited for them to come back for him.

The only thing is that the guy in the picture has eyelids and you can't see the Xs, which I feel is a major defining feature. The outfit and pose are magnificent, though.

Any-old-way, upvoted with no bias whatsoever. Nope. No sirree. None.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great, coming to read this got a spoiler. Thank you guys (nah, it's my fault). Anyways I have a problem with this line:

he will gain a youthful vitality

Basically, you miss the cold nature of the Foundation right here.

E: O man, The circus is getting a good spot in my heart right now, and probably I'm not the only one who wants to know more about the man with the upside-down face. This is really good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially thought this was going to be a Little Mister, but he does make a good addition to the circus. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite liked this one, mostly for the interview. Really says a lot without going on too long.

item.title: Quick Note
item.content:encoded: If you're having trouble visualizing the effect, think a mix between a slinky and a neon sign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good article, though I hope the Circus won't go the way of AWCY, with a ton of new articles that gets people tired of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm, a no vote for now although I did want to up vote for 'To have his hat back (Denied)' alone. I remain at no vote though because although I like the character and all of the ambiance and background stuff the effect that makes it an SCP just doesn’t do anything for me. It just doesn’t tie together well in my head. I may have another read later and revise my vote though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is a good addition to the Circus, Berggren. You've got an upvote.

In related news, Cinicage was the first to rate and comment on this article, and he feels accomplished. Sources speculate that he will now get off of the SCP Foundation wiki for the night, and go to sleep because he is tired as fuck.

EDIT: Now that I'm awake and my posts can be a little more coherent, I'll tell you what I enjoy about this.

I like Uriel's character, I think of him as very lively and kind.

I really like the interview log. Like Roget said, it does say alot, I feel it adds a little more to how the Circus looked 'backstage', if you will.

One thing I found a tad unnerving in the interview was this line:

SCP-1834: (chuckling) I don't know what you could mean, sir. My sight is fine.

(Five seconds of silence, during which SCP-1834 retracts his right eyestalk slowly.)

The way it's worded ("SCP-1834 retracts his right eyestalk slowly.") makes me feel uncomfortable, in a good way.
